title: polypodium australe subsp. azoricum
intro: Polypodium australe subsp. azoricum, also known as the Azores polypody, is a fern species that belongs to the genus Polypodium, which is a group of plants characterized by their fern-like leaves and ability to grow in a variety of environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Polypodium australe subsp. azoricum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce inflammation, making it a valuable remedy for conditions such as arthritis and high blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and used as a tea or infused in hot water to extract its medicinal properties.
